teams.name: Τμήμα Εκμάθησης
teams.description_en:
Playful Water Familiarization
Overcoming Fear of Water, Learning Breathing, Floating and Water Safety Skills
The 10 Most Frequently Asked Questions to the Coach
Answered by Stratos Malamadakis – Head Learn-to-Swim Coach
1. My child is afraid of the water. Can they still start swimming lessons?

Absolutely. Our initial goal is not for children to swim, but to feel safe and comfortable in the water. Every child adapts at their own pace, and we fully respect and support that individual journey.

2. What is the main goal of the learn-to-swim program?

Our goal is to help children develop confidence in the water, learn how to float, breathe correctly, and move independently and safely.

3. Do you use games during the lessons?

Yes. At young ages, play is one of the most effective learning tools. Through games, children become comfortable in the water and develop a genuine love for swimming.

4. How quickly will my child learn to swim?

There is no fixed timeline. Progress depends on the child's age, attendance frequency, personality, and individual learning pace.

5. How do you know when a child is ready to move on to the Academy level?

A child is ready when they can move independently in the water, follow instructions, and perform basic skills such as introductory freestyle and backstroke movements without flotation aids and with confidence and safety.

6. How important is proper technique from an early age?

It is extremely important. Good habits and proper technique are built from the very beginning and accompany children throughout their athletic journey. Swimming is one of the few sports in which technical foundations need to be developed early, primarily for safety reasons.

7. How many training sessions per week do you recommend?

For most children, two to three sessions per week provide the ideal balance for steady progress and skill development.

8. Should parents push their child to progress quickly?

No. Our priority is for children to enjoy swimming and look forward to every lesson. They will learn to love the sport through play, positive experiences, and the relationships they build with their teammates and coaches.

9. What is the most common mistake you see parents make?

Comparing their child to others. Every child develops at a different pace and may face challenges with different skills along the way.

10. What would you like a child to have gained by the end of the learn-to-swim program?

Confidence, safety, independence in the water, and a lifelong love for swimming and aquatic activities.

teams.name: Ακαδημία
teams.description_en: Teaching the four strokes (freestyle, backstroke, breaststroke, butterfly), starts & turns.
teams.description: Διδασκαλία των τεσσάρων στιλ (ελεύθερο, ύπτιο, πρόσθιο, πεταλούδα), εκκινήσεις & στροφές.

teams.name: Προαγωνιστικό
teams.description_en: Increased mileage, technique under fatigue, introduction to HSF events.
teams.description: Αύξηση χιλιομετρικού όγκου, τεχνική υπό κόπωση, εισαγωγή σε ημερίδες ΚΟΕ.

teams.name: Αγωνιστικό & Τεχνική
teams.description_en: Transition to Finswimming: monofin, bi-fins, apnea. Participation in Hellenic & International events.
teams.description: Μετάβαση στην Τεχνική Κολύμβηση: μονοπέδιλο, διπλά πέδιλα, άπνοια. Συμμετοχή σε Πανελλήνια & Διεθνή.
